question.text: A "Pre-COD project" under InvIT Regulations must have achieved completion of at least what percentage of construction OR expended not less than what percentage of total capital cost?

answer.text: 60% of construction or 40% of capital cost
75% of construction or 75% of capital cost
50% of construction or 50% of capital cost
25% of construction or 25% of capital cost

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A Pre-COD project is one that has not achieved commercial operation date but has either achieved completion of at least 50% of construction (as certified by an independent engineer) or expended not less than 50% of the total capital cost as per the financial package. **The Trap:** Both thresholds are the same at 50%, making this easier to remember but the distractors try to confuse with different percentage combinations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 50% threshold ensures Pre-COD projects in InvITs have meaningful progress, reducing speculative risk for unit holders.
